tm states [--json]

      One-line fleet snapshot: REPO, SID (first 8 chars), STATUS,
      BUSY (yes if the on-busy hook's .busy file is present), LAST
      (size + age of <sid>.last), PREVIEW (first 50 chars of last
      reply). Use it to see what every teammate is doing at a glance.
      STATUS is the liveness verdict:
        alive         session up, claude process up
        dead-session  the tmux session is gone (L1 death)
        dead-proc     session up but claude crashed back to a shell
                      (L2 death)
        maybe-wedged  claude up but its .busy marker is very stale —
                      a suspected wedge; reported only, never resumed
        starting      launched moments ago, still coming up
      The snapshot covers every live teammate session AND every repo
      with a /tmp/teammate-<repo>.sid file, so a teammate whose
      session vanished still shows up (as dead-session) instead of
      silently disappearing.
      --json emits a machine-readable array — one object per teammate
      with repo, sid, status, busy — for the fleet-health sweep cron
      and the dispatcher boot-recovery flow.
      Every run also records each repo's verdict to
      /tmp/teammate-<repo>.health, the independent observation that
      'tm resume --auto' checks before an automatic resume.
